message:
fix(parser): prevent clause-starting keywords from being consumed as implicit aliases

Adds is_alias_terminator_kw() guard to parse_optional_alias() so that
nonreserved keywords like FULL, RETURNING, JOIN, WHERE, GROUP, HAVING,
ORDER, LIMIT, UNION, INTERSECT, EXCEPT, etc. are not greedily consumed
as implicit alias names. Previously, queries like "FROM l FULL OUTER JOIN r"
would parse FULL as an alias for l instead of recognizing it as a join type.

Includes two regression tests:
- FULL OUTER JOIN parsing with semicolon termination
- INSERT ... RETURNING clause recognition

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
